_setup_note: Targets burstable T-type EC2 instances. Credits < 100 means you're burning through burst headroom.
name: [Rogers VI] EC2 CPU Credit Balance Low

_setup_note: Uses change() to detect a sudden increase in restarts rather than a raw count (which grows forever). Tune the threshold based on your baseline restart count.
name: [Rogers VI] Pod Restart Spike Detected

_setup_note: Mirrors the formula already in your dashboard widget. Alerts before disk full kills MongoDB writes or log rotation.
name: [Rogers VI] VM Disk Usage High

_setup_note: Same formula as dashboard. Warning at 80% gives time to react before OOM killer starts evicting processes.
name: [Rogers VI] VM Memory Usage High

_setup_note: 15-minute window prevents false positives from short bursts. Applies to all Rogers VI VMs.
name: [Rogers VI] VM CPU Utilization Sustained High

_setup_note: Load > 1.0 on system.load.15 (15-min avg) means the CPU run queue is consistently saturated. This is the threshold already marked on your dashboard widget.
name: [Rogers VI] MongoDB System Load Critical

_setup_note: Throttled seconds as a rate. High throttling = pods are consistently hitting their CPU limits, causing latency spikes in video processing. Tune threshold after observing your baseline.
name: [Rogers VI] K8s CPU Throttling Elevated

_setup_note: IMPORTANT: Replace <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME> with the actual service name(s) from your APM service catalog. Create one copy of this monitor per product (sdf, divabo, l2v, vi). The env tag may need to be 'prd' instead of 'production' — check your APM service map.
name: [Rogers VI] APM Service Error Rate High — <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME>

_setup_note: IMPORTANT: Replace <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME>. Thresholds are in nanoseconds (DD APM default). 500ms = 500000000ns, 2s = 2000000000ns. Adjust per service SLA — vi (video ingest) should probably be tighter (200ms warn / 500ms crit).
name: [Rogers VI] APM Service p99 Latency High — <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME>

_setup_note: Catches errors on outbound calls (DB, external APIs, CDN) before they cascade into your service error rate. Replace <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME>. The span.kind:client filter scopes to outbound calls only.
name: [Rogers VI] APM Downstream Dependency Error Rate — <YOUR_SERVICE_NAME>
